_comment: Profile 1: Blocks MORE syscalls than moby default, but ALLOWS more Tier 1 dangerous syscalls.
defaultAction blocks everything not listed. The allow list is deliberately smaller than moby
(fewer total allowed syscalls) BUT includes bpf and ptrace unconditionally.
- Coverage score: HIGHER than moby (more syscalls blocked)
- Dangerous Exposure score: MUCH LOWER (bpf + ptrace are Tier 1, weight ~6.67 each)
- Overall hardening score: LOWER than moby despite blocking more syscalls
This tests that seccompute correctly penalizes dangerous syscall exposure

syscalls._security_note: bpf weight=6.67, ptrace weight=6.67. Together ~13.3 points of exposure.

syscalls._comment: INTENTIONALLY ALLOWED: Two more Tier 1 syscalls for extra exposure.
syscalls._security_note: process_vm_readv + process_vm_writev: cross-process memory r/w. weight ~6.67 each.
